title: Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum
intro: Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum, commonly known as Lasioside, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum include lasioside, a triterpenoid saponin, as well as other saponins and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. The genus Neomanniophyton is part of the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. It is a member of the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that includes many species of flowers, trees, and shrubs. The classification of Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum is further grouped into the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants with a three-part flower. The kingdom to which Neomanniophyton lasiocarpum belongs is Plantae, which comprises all flowering and non-flowering plants.
